mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings contractions, causing investors to price in lower future cash flows and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and residential investment indicates robust consumer confidence and credit availability, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in backtests covering at least two full business cycles, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its historical trend level confirms strong aggregate supply capacity and healthy corporate utilization rates, supporting margin expansion and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO sustains levels above 105 for consecutive months, indicating the signal captures mean reversion rather than trend continuation.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP directly correlates with expanding corporate profit bases, leading to systematic positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings revisions move higher.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if real GDP growth decouples from equity returns for more than two consecutive quarters, or if the forward return distribution shows no positive skew during GDP expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates while the federal funds rate remains elevated, real interest rates turn deeply positive, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes during stagflation episodes where equities historically act as an inflation hedge, or forward returns remain positive despite rising real rates.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and declining unemployment confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which boosts consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved earnings momentum.
failure_condition: The signal fails when payroll additions exceed 250,000 monthly but equity returns turn negative due to wage-push inflation fears overriding growth optimism.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained drop in housing starts below 1.4 million annualized units signals a credit crunch and collapsing consumer wealth, which transmits to broader economic activity and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if equity markets rally despite prolonged housing starts below 1.4 million, indicating decoupling between real estate activity and corporate equity valuations.
